content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

January 9, 1991

Mr. Martin Eber  
EBER & NAKAGAWA  
650 California Street, 26th Floor  
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Phone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-744

Dear Mr. Eber:

By letter dated December 12, 1990, you have sought further advice on behalf of San Francisco Supervisor Richard Hongisto, regarding his duties and responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

The supervisor and Randy Riddle of the City Attorney's Office were advised by telephone on November 30, 1990 that it appeared the supervisor had a potential disqualifying conflict of interest on a pending condominium conversion ordinance before the Board of Supervisors, based on his ownership of a number of rental properties in the city.

We have been informed that a vote on the proposed ordinance was continued at a recent meeting of the board of supervisors, after which Mr. Hongisto stepped down from his position as supervisor and assumed the office of Assessor for the City and County of San Francisco. For this reason, we will not provide any further advice on this matter because the underlying facts giving rise to the request have become moot.

If you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman  
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
